_comment: Project-level MCP server configuration for Claude Architect Foundations. Demo anchor for COURSE-FLOW.md Segment 2. Three transports shown: stdio (local subprocess), HTTP (remote with API key), SSE (remote with bearer token). Env-var expansion via ${VAR} works in command, args, env values, url, and headers.

filesystem._comment: stdio transport. Local subprocess launched via npx. No auth needed because the server runs as your user with your file-system permissions. Scope the allowed paths in args, never expose the whole drive.

github._comment: stdio transport with secret. GITHUB_TOKEN expands from environment. The token never lands in git because the value lives in your shell env, not in this file.

context7._comment: HTTP transport. Remote MCP server. Header-based auth with API key. Use HTTP for stateless request-response servers; use SSE for streaming.

internal-knowledge-base._comment: SSE transport. Remote server with streaming responses. Bearer-token auth. Swap the URL for your team's internal MCP server in production.

oreilly-cca-mcp._comment: stdio transport. The course's own FastMCP demo server (examples/mcp_cli/mcp_server.py), the same DocumentMCP you run under `mcp dev` with the Inspector. Exposes all three MCP primitives: tools (read_doc_contents, edit_document), resources (docs://documents plus the templated docs://documents/{doc_id}), and a prompt (format). `uv run --directory` makes uv resolve the examples/mcp_cli/ project and its .venv, then runs the script, whose __main__ guard calls mcp.run(transport='stdio'). The path is absolute via ${PROJECT_ROOT} so the server starts the same way whether Claude Code launches from the repo root or a subdirectory. No auth: it runs as your user, in-process docs, no network.

cca-study-mcp._comment: stdio transport. The CCA Cert Buddy app's own MCP server (cca-cert-buddy/mcp-server/index.ts), a TypeScript counterpart to the Python oreilly-cca-mcp above. It exposes all three MCP primitives - tools (score_answer, validate_question, lookup_domain_ref), resources (cca://domains, cca://progress, cca://bank/{domain}, cca://domain-ref/{id}), and prompts (/quiz, /exam, /explain-domain). `cwd` is set to cca-cert-buddy/ so `node --import tsx` resolves the tsx package from that project's node_modules; tsx then runs the .ts entry directly. No auth: it runs as your user against the local question bank. Run `npm install` in cca-cert-buddy/ once before first use.
